result:
The cyclic dep error is pre-existing from a concurrent commit (`af4028f`), not from my fix. My change only edits `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs`.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs:1801` defined a stub `get_local_name` that always returned `None`, shadowing the real implementation in `crates/lua-vm/src/func.rs:647`. This is called by `basic_get_obj_name` (debug.rs:934) to look up local variable names for `OP_CALL` arguments. With the stub, `debug.getinfo(N).name` always degraded to `"?"` for any function called through a local. In `locals.lua` line 262, `assert(debug.getinfo(2).name == "foo")` (inside an inline `<close>` handler with foo as a global) failed because the level-2 frame's name resolution went through the broken stub.

**Fix:** Wire the debug.rs `get_local_name` to call through to `crate::func::get_local_name`. One-line change at debug.rs:1801-1805.

**Verified state:** With my reduced reproducer (foo as global, two `<close>` locals, level-2 lookup inside the __close handler), `debug.getinfo(2).name` now correctly returns `"foo"` instead of `"?"`. I did not re-run the full `locals.lua` test because a concurrent agent (commit `af4028f`) introduced a cyclic dep (`lua-vm → lua-gc → lua-vm` and a missing `heap` field) into `state.rs` between my reproducer run and the final build. That cycle is outside the scope of this debug task and unrelated to the `getinfo` fix.

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5; echo "exit=$?"
